office_does: The county council adopts county policy and budgets and oversees services in unincorporated Clark County.
race_blurb: Peter Silliman offers county charter and policy-analysis experience; Troy McCoy offers elected school-board, council, and mayoral experience. Both stress public safety, infrastructure, controlled spending, and voter consent for light rail.

candidates.summary: A county charter participant and former council policy analyst emphasizing checks and balances, budget transparency, taxpayer protection, and public safety.
highlights: Supports public-safety prioritization, budget transparency, and taxpayer protection [S1]
Says he sponsored charter amendments on public safety, budgets, ambiguity, and checks and balances [S1]
Also supports housing affordability, parks, trails, and infrastructure [S1]

spending.basis: Centers budget transparency, taxpayer protection, checks, and reduced government interference.

safety.basis: Explicitly prioritizes public safety but gives no detailed enforcement-versus-prevention mechanism.

experience.basis: Has deep county charter and council-office experience but no documented elected tenure.

candidates.summary: A former mayor and current municipal councilmember emphasizing managed growth, responsible budgets, public safety, infrastructure, and open administration.
highlights: Says taxpayers should not fund light rail without voter approval [S1]
Supports responsible budgets, managed growth, infrastructure, and public safety [S1]
Has served on a school board, city council, and as mayor [S1]

spending.basis: Stresses responsible budgets and rejects taxpayer-funded light rail absent voter approval.

safety.basis: Prioritizes public safety and adequately equipped emergency services without detailing a prevention-side program.

experience.basis: Offers elected school-board, city-council, and mayoral experience plus long small-business ownership.
